items.headline: European regulators propose power to ban foreign stablecoins over reserve risk
items.body: Germany and Italy drafted rules letting regulators ban stablecoins if reserve transfer mechanisms fail or issuers breach home-country rules. The proposal aims to prevent contagion from stablecoin collapse rippling through financial markets before regulatory window closes end-2026.

items.headline: Cuba releases over 2,000 prisoners as fuel blockade briefly eases
items.body: Cuba announced release of 2,010 prisoners, the largest such action in years, as island faces mounting pressure from fuel blockade. Trump administration tightened oil embargo attempting to force reforms but briefly allowed Russian tanker through this week.

items.headline: Israel deploys second division to southern Lebanon in ground invasion
items.body: Israeli forces launched ground operations toward Litani River, with officials saying campaign would resemble Gaza war and ruling out displaced residents returning. Over 1,300 killed in Lebanon since offensive began with more than one million displaced from conflict.

items.headline: Artemis II crew passes halfway point on first crewed lunar mission in 54 years
items.body: Four astronauts completed translunar injection burn sending spacecraft out of Earth orbit toward Moon on ten-day flyby mission. First crewed flight beyond low Earth orbit since Apollo 17 in 1972 tests systems for future lunar landing missions.

meanwhile.text: Charlotte hosts 17-day citywide arts festival with over 200 installations and performances
Researchers develop cleaner molten salt method for synthesizing ultra-thin MXene materials
GLP-1 medications reframed as longevity medicines reducing heart events and diabetes risk
Far-right parties now sit in third of OECD governments shaping international funding policies

es.headline: Spain makes mobile digital ID legally mandatory as phone replaces national card
es.body: Citizens can now use MiDNI app for in-person identification at banks, hotels, and government offices as legal requirement takes effect. All public and private organizations must accept the smartphone credential, restructuring how Spanish residents prove identity in daily transactions.

nl.headline: Major Dutch cities urge Senate to reject criminalization of illegal residence
nl.body: Amsterdam, Utrecht, Groningen, and Eindhoven warned Senate ahead of vote that making undocumented status a crime would drive vulnerable populations underground. Municipal officials argue enforcement burden and loss of community trust would undermine local safety and healthcare access.
